author: Gavriil GRIGOROV
title: Russian President Vladimir Putin has signed a law allowing those who fight in Ukraine to write off debts of up to nearly $100,000 | National | foxbangor.com
text: Russian President Vladimir Putin has signed a law allowing those who fight in Ukraine to write off debts of up to nearly $100,000 Gavriil GRIGOROV Nov 23, 2024 Nov 23, 2024 Updated 10 mins ago Facebook Twitter WhatsApp SMS Email Print Copy article link Save Russian President Vladimir Putin has signed a law allowing those who fight in Ukraine to write off debts of up to nearly $100,000 Gavriil GRIGOROV Tags Partners/afp As featured on +10 Putin vows more tests of nuke-capable missile fired at Ukraine Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday promised more combat test-firing of an experimental hypersonic missile launched at Ukraine, as Volodymyr Zelensky appealed for updated air-defence systems to meet the new threat. Putin signs law letting Ukraine fighters write off bad debts Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday signed a law that allows those who sign up to fight in Ukraine to write off unpaid debts worth almost $100,000, the government announced. Putin signs law letting Ukraine fighters write off bad debts Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday signed a law that allows those who sign up to fight in Ukraine to write off unpaid debts worth almost $100,000, the government announced.
